---
pagetitle: "Martin Lukk"
description: "Sociologist studying politics, inequality, and the internet."
page-layout: custom
listing:
  - id: news-recent
    contents: news/*.qmd
    exclude:
      filename: "index.qmd"
    type: custom
    template: _templates/news-item.ejs
    max-items: 4
    sort: "date desc"
    fields: [date, title]
    page-size: 4
---

I am a sociologist working at the intersection of *politics*, *inequality*, and *the internet*, and I am currently bridging this work into AI evaluation. I study how cultural divisions become sources of political conflict, and how digital platforms enable and constrain pro-social behaviour.

I am an author of the book [*GoFailMe: The Unfulfilled Promise of Digital Crowdfunding*](book/) (Stanford University Press, 2024), and I am building [**FairFund-Bench**](fairfund/) — a benchmark that audits how large language models allocate scarce resources in real crowdfunding scenarios.

I am a Postdoctoral Research Fellow at the [Data Sciences Institute](https://datasciences.utoronto.ca/), University of Toronto.

## News

::: {#news-recent}
:::

[See all News &rarr;](news/){.see-all}
